content.question: What is the causal effect size of early tracking on mean student achievement (versus correlation with selection factors)? Do students in selective tracks gain more absolute learning, or do they gain more *relative* to lower-track peers due to peer effects and resource concentration?
content.reduction_path: Conduct regression discontinuity or instrumental-variable analysis using track assignment thresholds, comparing students just above and below cutoff scores; cross-national replication of Hanushek & Wößmann design with Hungarian data; triangulate against teacher value-added and expenditure-per-student data to distinguish peer effects from resource effects
